summary: Regression-only run after Game.jsx refactor (1029 -> 265 lines), new MenuScreen/HUD/PauseScreen/GameOverScreen split, gameEngine.js extraction, useLeaderboardScores hook, server.py type-hint pass. Backend 20/20 pytest still PASS. Frontend regression: 99% PASS for all flows (menu loads with title/play/leaderboard/mute/menu-high-score, controls hint with green/red/yellow legend, play -> HUD -> pause via button -> resume -> P-key pause/resume -> quit, leaderboard fetch+render+close, spacebar from menu starts, natural gameover with all 8 panel elements, score=0 disables submit/share/download, share/download labels, player-name pre-fill from localStorage, high-score persists across reload, no console errors). One regression-class issue uncovered (was already present in iteration_2 but masked by force-clicks): the corner mute-toggle button is unclickable on every screen except 'playing' because all overlay screens (MenuScreen, PauseScreen, GameOverScreen, Leaderboard) use 'absolute inset-0 z-40' and intercept pointer events from the z-30 mute-toggle. M-key keyboard shortcut still works correctly so the feature is reachable, but the button click on the menu does NOT toggle sound for end users.

ui_bugs.component: MenuScreen / PauseScreen / GameOverScreen / Leaderboard (all overlays) vs. corner mute-toggle in Game.jsx
ui_bugs.issue: Mute-toggle button (z-30 corner control in Game.jsx, line ~212) is visually on top but FUNCTIONALLY BLOCKED by every overlay screen which all use 'absolute inset-0 z-40' with pointerEvents auto. document.elementFromPoint at the mute-toggle coords returns the MenuScreen overlay div. Clicking the mute-toggle on the menu/pause/gameover/leaderboard screens does NOTHING. Confirmed via Playwright: text stays at 'SOUND ON' after click; non-forced click times out with 'overlay div intercepts pointer events'. Only mid-game (no overlay rendered) does the mute button actually work. M keyboard shortcut still works as a workaround.

action_items: Fix mute-toggle click-through: simplest fix is to give the corner control wrapper a higher z-index than the overlays. In Game.jsx change the wrapper div from 'z-30' to 'z-50' (currently z-30 vs overlays at z-40), OR add `pointer-events-none` to each overlay's outermost div and `pointer-events-auto` on its inner content so the corner buttons remain hit-testable. Same fix would also rescue the in-play pause-button if any future overlay collision arises (currently pause-button is only rendered while phase==='playing' so it works, but the same z-30 vs z-40 pattern is fragile).
critical_code_review_comments: Game.jsx (265 lines): clean separation; phaseRef pattern correctly avoids rAF resubscription on phase change; rAF dep array still includes [gravityDir, handleDeath] which causes effect re-subscription on every gravity flip — minor inefficiency, not a bug, but consider using refs for those too to make the rAF loop a true single-subscription effect.

Game.jsx line 182: setGravityDir is only called when result.gravityDir !== gravityDir, but `gravityDir` here is the closure value at effect-mount time; since the effect re-subscribes on gravityDir change this still works, but pairing with the previous comment, if you move to a single-mount rAF you must read gravityDir from a ref too.

Z-INDEX/POINTER-EVENTS BUG (see frontend_issues): 4 separate overlay components each declare 'absolute inset-0 z-40'. Centralize this overlay shell into a tiny <Overlay> component AND have it use pointer-events: none on the wrapper with pointer-events: auto on its inner panel — this both DRYs the code and prevents future click-through regressions for global controls.
MenuScreen.jsx line 7, PauseScreen.jsx line 7, GameOverScreen.jsx line 94, Leaderboard.jsx line 106: identical 'absolute inset-0 z-40 ...' className — extract into shared overlay primitive.
audio.js, shareCard.js, native.js: catch blocks now log warnings — verified.

useLeaderboardScores.js: dedicated fetch hook is a clean extraction; ensure it cancels in-flight requests on unmount (AbortController) to avoid setState-after-unmount warnings if user closes leaderboard during slow network.
GameOverScreen disabled-state at score=0 is correctly enforced for submit-score-button, share-card-button, download-card-button (all three return disabled=true via DOM property check).

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (15/16 regression checks). Only failing check: mute-toggle button click on overlay screens is intercepted by overlay div. Keyboard M shortcut still works.

seed_data_creation: None — used existing leaderboard data and pre-seeded localStorage with gravshift_local_high=42 and gravshift_name=TESTPLAYER to verify persistence flows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_gravshift_api.py runs cleanly with `pytest -v` against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — keep it as the regression suite. The mute-toggle bug was missed in iteration_2 because the previous agent used force=True clicks (force bypasses Playwright's hit-test but the click event still goes to the topmost element at the coordinate, so the button was never actually toggled). Always verify mute-toggle by reading textContent of [data-testid="mute-toggle"] before vs after a non-forced click. The four overlay components (MenuScreen/PauseScreen/GameOverScreen/Leaderboard) all share the 'absolute inset-0 z-40' pattern; once E1 fixes the z-index/pointer-events, re-check all four screens for clickability of the mute-toggle. Other findings: persistence (high-score, player-name) works across page reload; natural game-over reachable in <8s of no input; spacebar from menu/gameover starts/restarts; P toggles pause; M toggles mute; no console errors during full flow.
rca of the issue:
ROOT CAUSE: Z-index + stacking-context inversion. Game.jsx renders the corner control bar (mute-toggle + pause-button) inside a div with class 'absolute top-4 right-4 z-30'. Each overlay screen (MenuScreen/PauseScreen/GameOverScreen/Leaderboard) renders its outermost wrapper as 'absolute inset-0 z-40 ...' with default pointer-events (auto). Since z-40 > z-30 and the overlay covers the full viewport, document.elementFromPoint(muteX, muteY) returns the overlay div, not the mute button. Browser fires click on the overlay (which has no handler), and the button's onClick never runs.

REPRO: 1) Open app at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. 2) Without starting game, click [data-testid="mute-toggle"]. 3) Observe text remains 'SOUND ON'. 4) Press M key — text flips to 'SOUND OFF' (proving the toggle logic itself is fine).

MITIGATION (one-line fix): In Game.jsx change `className="absolute top-4 right-4 z-30 flex items-center gap-2"` to `z-50`. OR (better, structural): give every overlay's outermost div `pointer-events-none` and its inner content panel `pointer-events-auto` so the corner controls remain hit-testable while the overlay is purely visual on the empty regions.
